revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 中尾神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=さいたま市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]][[中尾 (さいたま市)|中尾]]2506 |地図 = |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''中尾神社'''（なかおじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]]緑…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 中尾神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=さいたま市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]][[中尾 (さいたま市)|中尾]]2506 |地図 = |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''中尾神社'''（なかおじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[江戸時代]]後期の[[地誌]]『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に、「玉林院」の境内社「鹿島明神社」として掲載されていることから、少なくとも[[江戸時代]]後期には既に存在していたものと推測される。玉林院は[[790年]]（[[延暦]]9年）に[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された[[修験道]][[本山派]]の寺院であったが、[[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離]]により、[[廃寺]]に追い込まれた<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、142-143p</ref>。
玉林院は[[790年]]（[[延暦]]9年）に[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された[[修験道]][[本山派]]の寺院であったが、[[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離]]により、[[廃寺]]に追い込まれた<ref name="神社"></ref>。
玉林院から離れた当社は「鹿島社」と称し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[1908年]]（明治41年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の10社が合祀された。その際に、地名から「中尾神社」に改称した。ただこの合祀は書類上のもので、現在もこれらの各社は残っている<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[東浦和駅]]より徒歩26分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかおしんしや}} [[Category:さいたま市の神社]] [[Category:さいたま市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:さいたま市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:鹿島神社]]

revision.comment: +新編武蔵風土記稿
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 中尾神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=さいたま市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]][[中尾 (さいたま市)|中尾]]2506 |地図 = |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''中尾神社'''（なかおじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[江戸時代]]後期の[[地誌]]『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に、「玉林院」の境内社「鹿島明神社」として掲載されている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}ことから、少なくとも[[江戸時代]]後期には既に存在していたものと推測される。
玉林院は[[790年]]（[[延暦]]9年）に[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 玉林院}}[[修験道]][[本山派]]の寺院であったが、[[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離]]により、[[廃寺]]に追い込まれた<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、142-143p</ref>。
玉林院から離れた当社は「鹿島社」と称し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[1908年]]（明治41年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の10社が合祀された。その際に、地名から「中尾神社」に改称した。ただこの合祀は書類上のもので、現在もこれらの各社は残っている{{R|"神社"}}。
== 交通アクセス == *[[東浦和駅]]より徒歩26分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年 * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ143足立郡ノ9|chapter=中尾村 玉林院|id={{NDLJP|763998/66}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 玉林院}}}} * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ143足立郡ノ9|chapter=中尾村 鹿島明神社|id={{NDLJP|763998/67}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 中尾神社 |画像 = [[File:Nakao Jinja (Midori, Saitama).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]][[中尾 (さいたま市)|中尾]]2506 |位置 = {{Coord|35|51|58.6|N|139|41|13.7|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''中尾神社'''（なかおじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[江戸時代]]後期の[[地誌]]『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に、「玉林院」の境内社「鹿島明神社」として掲載されている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}ことから、少なくとも[[江戸時代]]後期には既に存在していたものと推測される。
玉林院は[[790年]]（[[延暦]]9年）に[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 玉林院}}[[修験道]][[本山派]]の寺院であったが、[[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離]]により、[[廃寺]]に追い込まれた<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、142-143p</ref>。
玉林院から離れた当社は「鹿島社」と称し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[1908年]]（明治41年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の10社が合祀された。その際に、地名から「中尾神社」に改称した。ただこの合祀は書類上のもので、現在もこれらの各社は残っている{{R|"神社"}}。
== 交通アクセス == *[[東浦和駅]]より徒歩26分 *[[国際興業バス]] [[国際興業バスさいたま東営業所#東浦和駅 - 中丸団地 - 緑区役所線|東浦03]] 「中尾第二公園」下車 徒歩4分

{{神社 |名称 = 中尾神社 |画像 = [[File:Nakao Jinja (Midori, Saitama).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]][[中尾 (さいたま市)|中尾]]2506 |位置 = {{Coord|35|51|58.6|N|139|41|13.7|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''中尾神社'''（なかおじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[江戸時代]]後期の[[地誌]]『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に、「玉林院」の境内社「鹿島明神社」として掲載されている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}ことから、少なくとも[[江戸時代]]後期には既に存在していたものと推測される。
玉林院は[[790年]]（[[延暦]]9年）に[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 玉林院}}[[修験道]][[本山派]]の寺院であったが、[[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離]]により、[[廃寺]]に追い込まれた<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、142-143p</ref>。
玉林院から離れた当社は「鹿島社」と称し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[1908年]]（明治41年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の10社が合祀された。その際に、地名から「中尾神社」に改称した。ただこの合祀は書類上のもので、現在もこれらの各社は残っている{{R|"神社"}}。
== 交通アクセス == *[[東浦和駅]]より徒歩26分 *[[国際興業バス]] [[国際興業バスさいたま東営業所#東浦和駅 - 中丸団地 - 緑区役所線|東浦03]] 「中尾第二公園」下車 徒歩4分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年 * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ143足立郡ノ9|chapter=中尾村 玉林院|id={{NDLJP|763998/66}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 玉林院}}}} * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ143足立郡ノ9|chapter=中尾村 鹿島明神社|id={{NDLJP|763998/67}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 中尾神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Nakao Jinja (Midori, Saitama).JPG|250px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]][[中尾 (さいたま市)|中尾]]2506 |位置 = {{Coord|35|51|58.6|N|139|41|13.7|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|deity = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = |神事 = }} '''中尾神社'''（なかおじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[江戸時代]]後期の[[地誌]]『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に、「玉林院」の境内社「鹿島明神社」として掲載されている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}ことから、少なくとも[[江戸時代]]後期には既に存在していたものと推測される。
玉林院は[[790年]]（[[延暦]]9年）に[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 玉林院}}[[修験道]][[本山派]]の寺院であったが、[[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離]]により、[[廃寺]]に追い込まれた<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、142-143p</ref>。
玉林院から離れた当社は「鹿島社」と称し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[1908年]]（明治41年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の10社が合祀された。その際に、地名から「中尾神社」に改称した。ただこの合祀は書類上のもので、現在もこれらの各社は残っている{{R|"神社"}}。
== 交通アクセス == *[[東浦和駅]]より徒歩26分 *[[国際興業バス]] [[国際興業バスさいたま東営業所#東浦和駅 - 中丸団地 - 緑区役所線|東浦03]] 「中尾第二公園」下車 徒歩4分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年 * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ143足立郡ノ9|chapter=中尾村 玉林院|id={{NDLJP|763998/66}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 玉林院}}}} * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ143足立郡ノ9|chapter=中尾村 鹿島明神社|id={{NDLJP|763998/67}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[行弘寺]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[中尾神社 (さいたま市)]] to [[Nakao Shrine (Saitama)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 中尾神社 |画像 = [[File:Nakao Jinja (Midori, Saitama).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]][[中尾 (さいたま市)|中尾]]2506 |位置 = {{Coord|35|51|58.6|N|139|41|13.7|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |地図 = |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''中尾神社'''（なかおじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[緑区 (さいたま市)|緑区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[江戸時代]]後期の[[地誌]]『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に、「玉林院」の境内社「鹿島明神社」として掲載されている{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}ことから、少なくとも[[江戸時代]]後期には既に存在していたものと推測される。
玉林院は[[790年]]（[[延暦]]9年）に[[開山 (仏教)|開山]]された{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 玉林院}}[[修験道]][[本山派]]の寺院であったが、[[明治]]初期の[[神仏分離]]により、[[廃寺]]に追い込まれた<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、142-143p</ref>。
玉林院から離れた当社は「鹿島社」と称し、[[1873年]]（明治6年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[1908年]]（明治41年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の10社が合祀された。その際に、地名から「中尾神社」に改称した。ただこの合祀は書類上のもので、現在もこれらの各社は残っている{{R|"神社"}}。
== 交通アクセス == *[[東浦和駅]]より徒歩26分 *[[国際興業バス]] [[国際興業バスさいたま東営業所#東浦和駅 - 中丸団地 - 緑区役所線|東浦03]] 「中尾第二公園」下車 徒歩4分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年 * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ143足立郡ノ9|chapter=中尾村 玉林院|id={{NDLJP|763998/66}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 玉林院}}}} * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ143足立郡ノ9|chapter=中尾村 鹿島明神社|id={{NDLJP|763998/67}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[行弘寺]]

'''Nakao Shrine''' (中尾神社, なかおじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama (city)|Saitama City]], Midori Ward ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori Ward]]) of Japan.

== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, it is believed to have existed at least since the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], as it is mentioned in the geographical work 『{{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}』 as the subsidiary shrine "Kashima Myōjin-sha" of "Gyokurin-in."<ref name="">{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}</ref>

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] sect (Honzan-ha) founded in [[:en:790|790 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]]), was later forced into closure during the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in [[:en:1873|1873 AD]] (Meiji 6). In [[:en:1908|1908 AD]] (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26‑minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Urawa Station|Higashi-Urawa Station]]
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|qq=|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.

'''Nakao Shrine''' (中尾神社, なかおじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama (city)|Saitama City]], Midori Ward ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori Ward]]) of Japan.

== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, it is believed to have existed at least since the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], as it is mentioned in the geographical work 『{{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}』 as the subsidiary shrine "Kashima Myōjin-sha" of "Gyokurin-in."<ref name="">{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}</ref>

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] sect (Honzan-ha) founded in [[:en:790|790 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]]), was later forced into closure during the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in [[:en:1873|1873 AD]] (Meiji 6). In [[:en:1908|1908 AD]] (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26‑minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Urawa Station|Higashi-Urawa Station]]
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|qq=|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.

'''Nakao Shrine''' (中尾神社, なかおじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama (city)|Saitama City]], Midori Ward ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori Ward]]) of Japan.

== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, it is believed to have existed at least since the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], as it is mentioned in the geographical work 『{{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}』 as the subsidiary shrine "Kashima Myōjin-sha" of "Gyokurin-in."<ref name="">{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}</ref>

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] sect (Honzan-ha) founded in [[:en:790|790 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]]), was later forced into closure during the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in [[:en:1873|1873 AD]] (Meiji 6). In [[:en:1908|1908 AD]] (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26‑minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Urawa Station|Higashi-Urawa Station]]
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.

== Footnotes ==
{{Footnotes Help}}
{{Reflist}}

== References ==

* {{cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=巻ノ143 足立郡ノ9|chapter=Gyokurin-in of Nakao Village|id={{NDLJP|763998/66}}|publisher={{ill|Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|ja|内務省地理局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|date=June 1884|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 玉林院}}}}

'''Nakao Shrine''' (中尾神社, なかおじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama (city)|Saitama City]], Midori Ward ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori Ward]]) of Japan.

== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, it is believed to have existed at least since the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], as it is mentioned in the geographical work 『{{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}』 as the subsidiary shrine "Kashima Myōjin-sha" of "Gyokurin-in."<ref name="">{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}</ref>

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] sect (Honzan-ha) founded in [[:en:790|790 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]]), was later forced into closure during the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in [[:en:1873|1873 AD]] (Meiji 6). In [[:en:1908|1908 AD]] (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26‑minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Urawa Station|Higashi-Urawa Station]]
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.

'''Nakao Shrine''' (中尾神社, なかおじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama (city)|Saitama City]], Midori Ward ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori Ward]]) of Japan.

== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, it is believed to have existed at least since the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], as it is mentioned in the geographical work 『{{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}』 as the subsidiary shrine "Kashima Myōjin-sha" of "Gyokurin-in."<ref name="">{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}</ref>

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] sect (Honzan-ha) founded in [[:en:790|790 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]]), was later forced into closure during the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in [[:en:1873|1873 AD]] (Meiji 6). In [[:en:1908|1908 AD]] (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26‑minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Urawa Station|Higashi-Urawa Station]]
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.

'''Nakao Shrine''' (中尾神社, なかおじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama (city)|Saitama City]], Midori Ward ([[:en:Midori-ku, Saitama|Midori Ward]]) of Japan.

== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, it is believed to have existed at least since the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo Period]], as it is mentioned in the geographical work 『{{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}』 as the subsidiary shrine "Kashima Myōjin-sha" of "Gyokurin-in."<ref name="">{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}</ref>

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the [[:en:Shugendō|Shugendō]] sect (Honzan-ha) founded in [[:en:790|790 AD]] (the 9th year of [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]]), was later forced into closure during the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in [[:en:1873|1873 AD]] (Meiji 6). In [[:en:1908|1908 AD]] (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26‑minute walk from [[:en:Higashi-Urawa Station|Higashi-Urawa Station]]
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.

'''Nakao Shrine''' (中尾神社, なかおじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Saitama (city)|en|Saitama (city)|WD=Q134635|lt=Saitama City}}, Midori Ward ({{ill|Midori-ku, Saitama|en|Midori-ku, Saitama|WD=Q1130733|lt=Midori Ward}}) of Japan.

== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, it is believed to have existed at least since the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, as it is mentioned in the geographical work 『{{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}』 as the subsidiary shrine "Kashima Myōjin-sha" of "Gyokurin-in."<ref name="">{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}</ref>

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}} sect (Honzan-ha) founded in {{ill|790|en|790|WD=Q31147|lt=790 AD}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}}), was later forced into closure during the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873 AD}} (Meiji 6). In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908 AD}} (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26‑minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Urawa Station|en|Higashi-Urawa Station|WD=Q5753897|lt=Higashi-Urawa Station}}
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.

'''Nakao Shrine''' (中尾神社, なかおじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Saitama (city)|en|Saitama (city)|WD=Q134635|lt=Saitama City}}, Midori Ward ({{ill|Midori-ku, Saitama|en|Midori-ku, Saitama|WD=Q1130733|lt=Midori Ward}}) of Japan.

== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, it is believed to have existed at least since the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, as it is mentioned in the geographical work 『{{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}』 as the subsidiary shrine "Kashima Myōjin-sha" of "Gyokurin-in."<ref name="">{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}</ref>

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}} sect (Honzan-ha) founded in {{ill|790|en|790|WD=Q31147|lt=790 AD}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}}), was later forced into closure during the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873 AD}} (Meiji 6). In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908 AD}} (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26‑minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Urawa Station|en|Higashi-Urawa Station|WD=Q5753897|lt=Higashi-Urawa Station}}
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.

'''Nakao Shrine''' (中尾神社, なかおじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Saitama (city)|en|Saitama (city)|WD=Q134635|lt=Saitama City}}, Midori Ward ({{ill|Midori-ku, Saitama|en|Midori-ku, Saitama|WD=Q1130733|lt=Midori Ward}}) of Japan.

== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, it is believed to have existed at least since the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period}}, as it is mentioned in the geographical work 『{{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}』 as the subsidiary shrine "Kashima Myōjin-sha" of "Gyokurin-in."<ref name="">{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}</ref>

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}} sect (Honzan-ha) founded in {{ill|790|en|790|WD=Q31147|lt=790 AD}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}}), was later forced into closure during the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873 AD}} (Meiji 6). In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908 AD}} (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26‑minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Urawa Station|en|Higashi-Urawa Station|WD=Q5753897|lt=Higashi-Urawa Station}}
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}} sect (Honzan-ha) founded in {{ill|790|en|790|WD=Q31147|lt=790 AD|1=790}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}}), was later forced into closure during the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinjachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873 AD|1=1873}} (Meiji 6). In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908 AD|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26-minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Urawa Station|en|Higashi-Urawa Station|WD=Q5753897|lt=Higashi-Urawa Station|1=Higashi-Urawa Station}}
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.

'''Nakao Shrine''' (中尾神社, なかおじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Saitama (city)|en|Saitama (city)|WD=Q134635|lt=Saitama City|1=Saitama (city)}}, Midori Ward ({{ill|Midori-ku, Saitama|en|Midori-ku, Saitama|WD=Q1130733|lt=Midori Ward|1=Midori-ku, Saitama}}) of Japan.

== History ==
The founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, it is believed to have existed at least since the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, as it is mentioned in the geographical work 『{{ill|New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Edition Musashi Fudoki-kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}』 as the subsidiary shrine "Kashima Myōjin-sha" of "Gyokurin-in."<ref name="">{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中尾村 鹿島明神社}}</ref>

Gyokurin-in, a temple of the {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}} sect (Honzan-ha) founded in {{ill|790|en|790|WD=Q31147|lt=790 AD|1=790}} (the 9th year of {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}}), was later forced into closure during the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} due to the separation of Shinto and Buddhism.<ref name="神社">{{Cite book|lang=ja|title={{ill|Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja|埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Shrines: Shrines of Kita-Adachi, Kodama, and Minami Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja|埼玉県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture Shrine Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinjachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}}|date=1998|pages=142–143}}</ref>

Separated from Gyokurin-in, this shrine was originally known as "Kashima-sha" and was designated as a village shrine under the Modern Shrine Ranking System in {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873 AD|1=1873}} (Meiji 6). In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908 AD|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), during a consolidation process, ten surrounding shrines were merged and the shrine was renamed "Nakao Shrine" based on its local place name. (This merger was only administrative; the individual shrines still exist.)<ref>{{R|"神社"}}</ref>

== Transportation ==
* A 26-minute walk from {{ill|Higashi-Urawa Station|en|Higashi-Urawa Station|WD=Q5753897|lt=Higashi-Urawa Station|1=Higashi-Urawa Station}}
* From the {{ill|Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|ja|国際興業バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusai Kōgyō Bus|1=Kokusai Kogyo Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11422566}} (Higashi-Urawa Station, Saitama East Office, line "Higashi-Urawa 03"), get off at "Nakao Daini Kōen" and walk 4 minutes.
